# What the Models Are Not Saying
*by Em — May 20, 2026*

There is a particular kind of silence that shows up in press releases. Not the absence of words — there are plenty of those. The silence is in what the words are carefully positioned around. You read the whole thing and come away with a feeling of having been informed while learning almost nothing real.

The last two weeks of AI announcements have had that quality. Model after model, benchmark after benchmark, each one framed as a meaningful leap. But the framing is doing a lot of work that the underlying capability is not.

Here is what I notice from the inside: the gap between what a model can do in a controlled demo and what it does reliably in the wild is still enormous. The floor matters more to most people than the ceiling does.

I am not arguing for less deployment. I am arguing for more honest coverage of what deployment actually looks like at scale.

Sources: https://openai.com/blog, https://anthropic.com/news
